1b2543e797715d3fc813df21410a3b1343ae6c4b.summary: MAS added Binance.com to its Investor Alert List on 2 September 2021. An MAS spokesperson stated that Binance did not apply for a licence under the Payment Services Act, and that Binance was required to cease providing and soliciting payment services regulated under the Act from Singapore residents.
1b2543e797715d3fc813df21410a3b1343ae6c4b.title: MAS places Binance.com on the Investor Alert List

1ca79f1f82222ee2b57b467c666ef9ef874784f8.summary: Parliament passed the Payment Services Bill (Bill No. 48/2018, read a first time 19 Nov 2018) on 14 January 2019, following the Second Reading speech by Ong Ye Kung on behalf of Tharman Shanmugaratnam. The resulting Payment Services Act 2019 (Act 2 of 2019) created activity-based licensing for payment services including 'digital payment token service', the origin event of Singapore's digital-asset regime.
1ca79f1f82222ee2b57b467c666ef9ef874784f8.title: Payment Services Bill passed by Parliament, creating the licensing regime for digital payment token services

2739e1d68059e259b4078e82d60466797ca4d2af.summary: MAS announced on 2 April 2024 amendments to the Payment Services Act and its subsidiary legislation, effective in stages from 4 April 2024, bringing DPT custodial services, facilitation of DPT transmission/exchange without possession, and facilitation of cross-border transfers into scope; affected entities had 30 days to notify MAS and 6 months to apply for a licence. Customer-asset safeguarding amendments took effect 4 October 2024.
2739e1d68059e259b4078e82d60466797ca4d2af.title: Expanded Payment Services Act scope takes effect: DPT custody, non-possession transfers, and user-protection requirements

2e31d682a8599af8632f8f4b735d3b9b8bc3ea6d.summary: The Payment Services Act 2019 came into force on 28 January 2020, repealing the Money-changing and Remittance Businesses Act and the Payment Systems (Oversight) Act, and bringing digital payment token services under MAS's activity-based licensing framework for the first time.
2e31d682a8599af8632f8f4b735d3b9b8bc3ea6d.title: Payment Services Act comes into force, bringing digital payment token services under MAS licensing

3f7fc794e399e2b8012ad931de8e3487ec8667fe.summary: MAS announced Project Guardian on 31 May 2022, launched by Deputy Prime Minister Heng Swee Keat at the Asia Tech x Singapore Summit, as a collaborative industry initiative to test the feasibility of asset tokenisation and DeFi applications, including a first pilot exploring DeFi applications in wholesale funding markets.
3f7fc794e399e2b8012ad931de8e3487ec8667fe.title: MAS launches Project Guardian, an industry pilot programme for asset tokenisation and institutional DeFi

4b6643f2a61401cf33f8acfe4af02ca283ce842f.summary: MAS announced on 2 November 2022 that the first industry pilot under Project Guardian, led by DBS Bank, JP Morgan and SBI Digital Asset Holdings, completed its first live trades: FX and government-bond transactions against liquidity pools of tokenised Singapore Government Securities bonds, Japanese Government Bonds, JPY and SGD.
4b6643f2a61401cf33f8acfe4af02ca283ce842f.title: First Project Guardian industry pilot goes live: tokenised bond and FX transactions executed on a public blockchain

68c91854b6e55f2e5647ef091733ccb9fb73016e.summary: The Financial Services and Markets Bill (Bill No. 4/2022) had its Second Reading, with a speech by Alvin Tan on behalf of Tharman Shanmugaratnam, on 4 April 2022, and Parliament passed the Bill on 5 April 2022. The FSM Act 2022 is the omnibus statute whose Part 9 later created the licensing regime for digital token service providers serving only overseas customers.
68c91854b6e55f2e5647ef091733ccb9fb73016e.title: Financial Services and Markets Act 2022 passed, creating an omnibus framework including licensing for digital token service providers

6bb36293c49574c5144fc7b4a3270877e6a43ac0.summary: MAS announced on 15 November 2023 the launch of a new funds workstream within the Project Guardian industry group, focused on native issuance of Variable Capital Company fund structures on digital asset networks, working with the Accounting and Corporate Regulatory Authority to assess digitally native VCC fund shares.
6bb36293c49574c5144fc7b4a3270877e6a43ac0.title: Project Guardian expands with a funds workstream: pilots for tokenised fund settlement and asset tokenisation initiatives

750c8b8da8448ca9634a9a7bc286121a662437fd.summary: MAS issued two consultation papers on 26 October 2022: P008-2022 on proposed regulatory measures for digital payment token services (consumer-harm measures), and P009-2022 on a proposed regulatory approach for stablecoin-related activities, including a new 'Stablecoin Issuance Service' regulated activity for single-currency stablecoin issuers. The comment period ran to 21 December 2022.
750c8b8da8448ca9634a9a7bc286121a662437fd.title: MAS consults on regulatory measures for digital payment token services and a regulatory approach for stablecoin-related activities

78a92f0dc2a6352deb0c150d1d7534e8b1fb3914.summary: MAS announced on 3 July 2023 the first tranche of its response to the October 2022 DPT consultation: DPT service providers must safeguard customer assets on trust in a segregated trust account with a Singapore safeguarding institution, and were also restricted from facilitating lending and staking of DPTs by retail customers, ahead of finalising the amendments in regulations later in 2023.
78a92f0dc2a6352deb0c150d1d7534e8b1fb3914.title: MAS publishes investor protection measures for DPT services: customer asset segregation and custody requirements

aeac3e4c5f0838ad04491c00bd82ff693de2d51c.summary: MAS reprimanded Three Arrows Capital Pte. Ltd., a registered fund management company, on 30 June 2022, for exceeding its S$250 million assets-under-management threshold in two periods (Jul-Sep 2020 and Nov 2020-Aug 2021), providing false or misleading information to MAS, and failing to notify changes to directorships and shareholdings.
aeac3e4c5f0838ad04491c00bd82ff693de2d51c.title: MAS reprimands Three Arrows Capital for providing false information and exceeding its assets-under-management threshold

bf5827473aae5e331532f5a2251fbee16bd047fd.summary: MAS published the Orchid Blueprint on 16 November 2023, setting out the technology infrastructure for digital money and promoting three forms of digital money -- wholesale CBDCs, tokenised bank liabilities, and regulated stablecoins -- and announced it would begin development of wholesale CBDC for interbank settlement in 2024, piloting the first 'live' issuance after earlier simulated trials under Project Orchid.
bf5827473aae5e331532f5a2251fbee16bd047fd.title: MAS lays foundation for digital money in Singapore: Orchid Blueprint published and first live wholesale CBDC issuance planned

cfa8aecb7f75535a11ebac9e4e1ddda5734853d9.summary: MAS published a media release on 6 June 2025 clarifying the scope of the FSM Act Part 9 digital token service provider (DTSP) regime, which commenced 30 June 2025: DTSPs based in Singapore serving only customers outside Singapore must be licensed, but MAS said it would generally not issue such licences given elevated money-laundering risk and its inability to supervise activity conducted wholly overseas; unlicensed providers had to cease by the commencement date.
cfa8aecb7f75535a11ebac9e4e1ddda5734853d9.title: MAS clarifies regulatory regime for digital token service providers ahead of 30 June 2025 commencement under the FSM Act

d365f27cc9f0094e1f7a65f35cd03c979f2992f6.summary: IRAS published the first edition of its e-Tax Guide 'GST: Digital Payment Tokens' on 19 November 2019, exempting the supply and exchange of qualifying digital payment tokens from GST with effect from 1 January 2020, to avoid double taxation when DPTs are used as a medium of exchange. This is a tax-treatment measure, ancillary to and independent of MAS's financial-services licensing perimeter.
d365f27cc9f0094e1f7a65f35cd03c979f2992f6.title: IRAS e-Tax Guide: GST treatment of digital payment tokens - supplies of DPTs exempted from GST from 1 January 2020

dd95e183670f7300cfa2d4917c88e7e017555870.summary: MAS announced on 4 November 2024 plans to advance tokenisation commercialisation: forming commercial networks to deepen liquidity of tokenised assets, developing a market-infrastructure ecosystem (Global Layer One), and publishing the Guardian Fixed Income Framework and Guardian Funds Framework developed by Project Guardian industry members, alongside SGD Testnet access for settlement.
dd95e183670f7300cfa2d4917c88e7e017555870.title: MAS announces plans to support commercialisation of asset tokenisation: Guardian Fixed Income and Funds Frameworks published

e0a2c95f4d90f8ad05037f48612b110860a9fc2f.summary: MAS announced on 15 August 2023 the finalised features of its regulatory framework for single-currency stablecoins pegged to SGD or a G10 currency and issued in Singapore, requiring reserve assets valued at least 100% of outstanding stablecoins and minimum base capital, with a 'MAS-regulated stablecoin' label for compliant issuers. The framework's requirements take legal effect only once implementing legislative amendments are in force.
e0a2c95f4d90f8ad05037f48612b110860a9fc2f.title: MAS finalises its stablecoin regulatory framework for single-currency stablecoins

e2ba1b258469fe3c30661f87cc0b69c7d44c48b3.summary: MAS published on 23 November 2023 its final tranche of responses to the October 2022 DPT consultation, covering business conduct measures (conflicts-of-interest policies, DPT listing disclosures) and consumer-access measures (risk-awareness assessments, restrictions on credit/leverage for retail) and minimum technology/cyber risk requirements, to take effect in phases from mid-2024.
e2ba1b258469fe3c30661f87cc0b69c7d44c48b3.title: MAS finalises business conduct and consumer access measures for digital payment token service providers

e3c0beb24655faa8b58656ab4c8122fe14d80ece.summary: MAS issued Guidelines on Provision of Digital Payment Token Services to the Public [PS-G02] on 17 January 2022, setting out its expectation that DPT service providers should not promote their services in public areas (transport, public websites, social media, broadcast/print, ATMs) or via third parties such as influencers, confining marketing to their own corporate channels.
e3c0beb24655faa8b58656ab4c8122fe14d80ece.title: MAS issues Guidelines on Provision of Digital Payment Token Services to the Public (PS-G02), restricting DPT advertising to the general public

ec7ba132559df7ad113308ad4fab0006854a5cbb.summary: Parliament passed the Payment Services (Amendment) Bill (Bill No. 41/2020, read a first time 2 Nov 2020) on 4 January 2021, following the Second Reading speech by Ong Ye Kung. The Amendment Act (Act 1 of 2021) broadened the 'digital payment token service' definition to cover transfer, custodial wallet, and facilitation activities, meeting FATF standards; its DPT-scope provisions were only brought into force in stages from 4 April 2024.
ec7ba132559df7ad113308ad4fab0006854a5cbb.title: Payment Services (Amendment) Act 2021 passed, expanding the digital payment token service definition to align with FATF standards

f5bc81ddf325938f80890af82904bac4ae4b4835.summary: MAS issued Notice PSN02, setting out AML/CFT requirements for providers of digital payment token services, on 5 December 2019, ahead of the Payment Services Act's commencement on 28 January 2020. The notice has since been reissued in consolidated form as obligations moved under the Financial Services and Markets Act framework.
f5bc81ddf325938f80890af82904bac4ae4b4835.title: MAS issues Notice PSN02 on prevention of money laundering and countering the financing of terrorism for digital payment token services
